description_long: This object is a vintage-style deep-sea diving helmet, constructed from dark, metallic material with a rounded dome and a wide, flared collar at the base. The helmet features several circular glass portholes, including a prominent one at the front and two on the sides, all reinforced with metal grilles. There are multiple protruding fittings and bolts around the collar and on the dome, suggesting robust construction for underwater use. The surface is slightly weathered, giving it an aged, industrial appearance, and the glass portholes have a blue tint. The overall design is utilitarian, with no decorative elements, focusing on functionality and durability. This helmet would likely be seen as a collectible or display item in an urban context, such as a museum, themed restaurant, or private garage.
description: A vintage, dark metallic deep-sea diving helmet with blue-tinted glass portholes and a wide, riveted collar. The helmet has a weathered, industrial look and multiple reinforced fittings.
description_view_0: Side view showing the rounded dome, a large circular porthole with a blue grille, and the flared collar with visible bolts.
description_view_1: Rear view highlighting the smooth, rounded back of the helmet and the wide, riveted collar with no visible portholes.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, with a circular porthole and the same industrial features.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the main circular porthole with a blue grille, two side portholes, and the top fitting, all set above the wide, riveted collar.
